attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks (5-50km from finish) on mixed terrain or technical finales, exploiting opponent stalemates rather than pure climbing power. Prefers early breakaway infiltration over late-race sprints when possible.
team_tactics: Minimal team setup visible in wins—thrives as opportunist in breakaways or when rivals mark each other. Team used defensively to control climbs when protecting GC/stage lead (Tour de Pologne 2017).
terrain_preference: Excels on technical descents, cobblestones, wet circuits, and attritional stages without massive climbs. Vulnerable on sustained high-mountain finishes where pure climbing power decides outcomes.
vulnerability: Overextends with excessive front work when not tactically disciplined, leaving nothing for finale. Struggles when forced to follow pure climbers on extended mountain stages.
key_patterns: Long solo breakaway wins (5-50km): 4 documented victories using early attacks or bridge-and-counter moves
Superior descending skills: repeatedly mentioned as sealing victories after attacking on technical terrain
Early breakaway infiltration: observed in 5+ races across Catalunya, Tour, Giro stages
Tactical intelligence over physiology: attacks before climbs as heavy rouleur, exploits rival standoffs
Exceptional versatility: wins from breakaways, sprints from small groups, leads out sprinters, domestique work
Wet/technical conditions favored: attacks before organized sprints on circuits, wins on cobbles/descents
form_trajectory: neutral: dominant 2021 Tour (2 stage wins) but 2022 E3 showed team setup without personal payoff; no recent win data to confirm current level
